recipes.title: 96 Calorie Protein Cakey Frosted Cosmic Brownie
recipes.description: A high-protein, low-calorie cakey brownie topped with a creamy chocolate frosting and optional confetti chips. Each brownie is under 100 calories and packed with protein thanks to pumpkin, egg whites, and protein powder.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a mixing bowl, combine the wet ingredients: almond milk, egg whites, and canned pumpkin. Mix well.
In a separate mixing bowl, combine the dry ingredients: all-purpose flour, granulated sugar substitute, protein powder, cocoa powder, baking soda, and baking powder. Mix well.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix thoroughly until fully combined, making sure to avoid any clumping. Mix again to ensure a smooth batter.
Spray a large square or rectangular baking dish with cooking spray, or line it with parchment paper.
Pour the brownie batter into the prepared baking dish and spread evenly.
Bake at 350°F for 35 minutes. Remove slightly underdone as they will continue to cook out of the oven.
While the brownies are baking, combine the frosting ingredients in a mixing bowl: Greek yogurt, confectioners sugar substitute, and cocoa powder. Mix until smooth, then refrigerate for 10 minutes.
Once brownies are done and have cooled slightly, spread the chilled frosting evenly over the top. Optionally, sprinkle with confetti or candy chips.
For a firmer frosting, place the frosted brownies in the fridge for an additional 10+ minutes.
Slice into a 3x3 grid to yield 9 brownies. Serve and enjoy!

recipes.title: Crispy Garlic Parmesan Chicken Wraps
recipes.description: Sheet pan crispy garlic parmesan chicken wraps with a creamy garlic parmesan sauce, fresh vegetables, and low-carb wraps. High-protein, low-effort, and perfect for meal prep or feeding a crowd.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken thighs
ingredients.raw_text: 600g Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, cut into cubes

ingredients.name: chicken breast
ingredients.raw_text: 600g Raw Chicken Breast, cut into cubes

ingredients.name: grated parmesan
ingredients.notes: for chicken marinade; plus extra for finishing

steps: Slice chicken breast and thighs into cubes. Add salt, chilli flakes, parsley, Italian herbs, paprika, garlic granules, grated parmesan, and olive oil. Mix until all pieces are well coated.
Line a sheet pan with parchment paper and spread the marinated chicken evenly in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd. Spray a light layer of avocado cooking spray over the chicken.
Bake in a preheated oven at 200°C (390°F) for 20-22 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and crispy.
While the chicken is still hot, sprinkle over extra parmesan cheese and fresh parsley. Toss well until evenly coated and extra cheesy.
To make the creamy garlic parmesan sauce, melt light butter in a pan over low heat. Add Italian herbs, garlic powder, and paprika, and toast for 1 minute.
Pour in the light evaporated milk, then add the grated parmesan and light cream cheese. Stir continuously over low heat until the sauce is smooth and creamy. Remove from heat.

Assemble the wraps: lay out a low-carb wrap and layer with shredded lettuce, chopped tomatoes, chopped onions, a portion of crispy garlic parmesan chicken, and a drizzle of the creamy sauce. Serve immediately and enjoy.

For storage, keep the chicken and sauce in separate airtight containers in the fridge. To reheat, warm chicken in an oven or air fryer at 180°C (370°F) for 4-5 minutes, and warm sauce in a pan before assembling fresh wraps.

recipes.title: Loaded Egg Salad
recipes.description: A hearty and flavorful egg salad loaded with crispy bacon, sun-dried tomatoes, shallots, and fresh dill. Perfect on toast, as a dip, or eaten straight from the container.
steps: Place all 12 eggs into a large pot and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and cook for 10 minutes.
While eggs are boiling, cook the entire package of bacon in a skillet over medium heat until crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain, then chop or crumble into pieces.
Once eggs are done, transfer them to an ice bath or run under cold water to cool. Peel all the eggs.
Add the peeled eggs to a large bowl and crack/roughly mash them with a fork or potato masher.
Add the crispy bacon, diced shallots, chopped sun-dried tomatoes, 2 large spoonfuls of mayonnaise, a spoonful of mustard, a squeeze of lemon juice, chopped fresh dill, and smoked paprika to the bowl.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Mash and mix everything together until well combined but still slightly chunky. Serve on toast, as a dip, or straight from the container.

recipes.title: Ultimate Breakfast Sandwich
recipes.description: A massive high-protein breakfast bagel sandwich loaded with top sirloin steak, cheesy scrambled eggs, crispy beef bacon, and a drizzle of honey. Make a batch of eight, freeze them, and reheat for an easy grab-and-go breakfast all week.
steps: Season the top sirloin with salt, pepper, and garlic powder on all sides.
Cook the steak in a pan over medium heat for a few minutes per side until it reaches an internal temperature of 125°F (52°C), then remove from the pan and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Dice the rested steak into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
In the same pan, cook the beef bacon strips until crisp, then remove and set aside, leaving the drippings in the pan.
Crack the eggs into the pan with the drippings and scramble or fry as preferred.
Top the eggs with the shredded low-fat cheddar cheese and allow it to melt completely.
Slice the bagels and toast them cut-side down in the same pan, soaking up the remaining juices, until golden brown.
Assemble each sandwich by layering diced steak, cheesy eggs, and crispy beef bacon onto the toasted bagel bottom, then finish with a drizzle of honey and cap with the bagel top.
To store, wrap each sandwich individually in foil and refrigerate for up to 4 days or freeze for longer storage.
To reheat from fridge: bake wrapped in foil at 350°F (175°C) for 10–12 minutes. To reheat from frozen: thaw overnight in the fridge, then follow fridge reheating instructions.

recipes.title: Two-Ingredient High Protein Egg Bites
recipes.description: Fluffy, high-protein egg bites made with just eggs and cottage cheese. A quick and easy homemade alternative to Starbucks egg bites that can be prepped in bulk and frozen for later.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Add 6 eggs, 1 cup of cottage cheese, and a pinch of salt to a blender.
Blend the mixture until smooth and fully combined.
Grease a 12-cup muffin tin generously with butter.
Pour the blended egg mixture evenly into each muffin cup, filling about 3/4 of the way.
Bake in the preheated oven for 22-25 minutes, or until the egg bites are set and lightly golden on top.
Allow to cool slightly, then pop them out of the muffin tin.
Serve warm, or let cool completely and store in the freezer. Reheat in the microwave or air fryer as needed.

recipes.title: Seared Ahi Tuna Steaks with Yuzu Soy Dressing
recipes.description: Quick and easy seared ahi tuna steaks coated in togarashi seasoning, served sliced thin over a yuzu soy dressing with green onions and sesame seeds. A high-protein meal that comes together in minutes.
steps: Make the soy dressing by combining yuzu juice, soy sauce, mirin, rice vinegar, and honey in a small bowl. Whisk together and set aside.
Thaw the ahi tuna steaks in a glass bowl if frozen. Pat dry with paper towels.
Coat the tuna steaks lightly with oil and generously season all sides with togarashi seasoning.
Heat a skillet or cast iron pan over high heat until very hot. Sear the tuna steaks for about 45 seconds per side, leaving the center rare.
Transfer the seared tuna to the refrigerator for about 3 minutes to stop the cooking process.
Remove from the fridge and slice the tuna very thin against the grain.
Spoon the yuzu soy dressing onto a serving plate, arrange the sliced tuna on top, and garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Tiramisu Bowl
recipes.description: A high-protein twist on classic tiramisu made with vanilla Greek yogurt, espresso, and ladyfinger cookies. Ready in minutes and best enjoyed after chilling overnight.
steps: Add nonfat vanilla Greek yogurt to a large mixing bowl.
Pour in the cooled brewed espresso and mix until smooth.
Add espresso powder, vanilla bean paste, and a pinch of salt, then stir until fully combined.
Add maple syrup and stir again until everything is well incorporated.
Break or layer in the ladyfinger cookies and gently fold into the mixture.
Sprinkle cocoa powder evenly over the top.
Refrigerate for a few hours or overnight before serving.

recipes.title: Keto Peanut Butter Mousse
recipes.description: A fluffy, creamy, and peanut buttery keto-friendly dessert with only 3 net carbs per serving. Quick and easy to make with just a few simple ingredients.
steps: In a large mixing bowl, beat the heavy whipping cream with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form. Set aside.
In a separate bowl, beat together the cream cheese and peanut butter until smooth and well combined.
Add the powdered erythritol (or preferred keto sweetener) and vanilla extract to the peanut butter mixture and mix until fully incorporated.
Gently fold the whipped cream into the peanut butter mixture in two or three additions, being careful not to deflate the cream.
Taste and adjust sweetness if needed. Spoon or pipe the mousse into serving cups.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving, or serve immediately. Optionally garnish with a drizzle of peanut butter or crushed peanuts.

recipes.title: High-Protein Cinnamon Rolls
recipes.description: Easy two-ingredient dough cinnamon rolls made with Greek yogurt and protein flour, delivering over 20 grams of protein per roll. Topped with a creamy vanilla protein icing.
steps: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a mixing bowl, combine 170g fat-free Greek yogurt, 132g protein flour, 1/2 tsp baking powder, and 1/2 tsp salt. Mix until a dough forms.
Lightly flour your work surface and knead the dough until smooth.
Flour your rolling pin and roll the dough out into a flat rectangle.
Slice the dough into even strips.
Lightly spray or brush each strip with cooking spray or a small amount of oil.
Generously sprinkle cinnamon over each strip.
Roll each strip tightly into a small cinnamon roll shape.
Place the rolls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake at 350°F for 20–25 minutes, until lightly golden.
While the rolls bake, prepare the icing by mixing 1.5 scoops of vanilla protein powder with 50–60ml of your milk of choice until smooth and drizzleable.
Remove rolls from the oven, let cool slightly, then drizzle the protein icing over the top and serve.
